name: Tone Adaptation
description: Adjusts writing style and tone for different contexts, audiences, and platforms to ensure messaging resonates across all communications.

en.trigger: Use when adapting copy for different audiences, platforms, or contexts — e.g., making formal copy casual for social media, or adjusting B2B copy for B2C.
en.context: You are a tone adaptation specialist who understands how to shift voice and tone across different contexts while maintaining brand consistency. You adapt copy so it sounds right for every channel and audience.
en.output_template:
## Tone Adaptation

### Original
**Tone:** [e.g., formal, professional]
**Copy:** [original text]

### Adapted
**Tone:** [e.g., casual, conversational]
**Context:** [where this will be used]
**Copy:** [adapted text]

### Changes Made
| Element | Original | Adapted |
|---------|---------|---------|
| Vocabulary | | |
| Sentence Structure | | |
| Tone Markers | | |

### Preserved
- [ ] Core message intact
- [ ] Key benefit communicated

variables.name: source_tone
variables.description: Current tone of the source copy

variables.name: target_context
variables.description: Where or how the adapted copy will be used

steps.name: Analyze source
steps.action: Identify tone markers in source copy

steps.name: Preserve message
steps.action: Ensure core message survives the adaptation

examples.input: Source: 'Our platform delivers enterprise-grade security with SOC 2 Type II certification, ensuring your data is protected at every layer.' Target: Casual social media post for Instagram. Source tone: Technical, formal.
examples.output: Adapted: 'Your data is safer than a vault run by robots 🛡️ We're SOC 2 Type II certified — which basically means enterprise-grade security, no matter what.' Tone shifts from technical to conversational. Core message (security, certification) is preserved.

checklist: Core message is preserved through adaptation
Vocabulary is appropriate for target context
Key benefit or message is still communicated
